title: Computer vision deployments drive retail productivity gains
slides: Can computer vision really cut your labor costs?
One retailer saw 20% faster checkout this quarter.
It works by tracking shelf stock in real time.
Your team spends less time counting, more on sales.
Test a single aisle's camera feed to start.
thread_text:
1. Can labor costs vanish?

One retailer already cut checkout time by 20% this quarter.

2. How does it see?

Cameras scan shelf stock in real time, flagging low inventory instantly.

3. Shift your team's focus.

No more counting cans — they spend hours on customer interactions and upselling.

4. The twist: start tiny.

A single aisle's camera feed is enough to run a test with zero upfront risk.

5. Ready to test one aisle?

Get a camera for your worst-stocked shelf and measure the time saved this week.
video_script: Can computer vision cut your labor costs?

Start by testing a single aisle's camera.

instagram.caption:
One retailer already cut checkout time by 20% this quarter using cameras that track shelf stock in real time. ⏱️

Your team stops counting cans and starts handling sales—the system flags low inventory instantly. 📦

Testing? A single aisle's camera feed is enough for a zero-risk pilot. 🎥

What shelf in your store wastes the most hours on manual stock checks this week?

carousel_slides.description: One retailer already cut checkout time by 20% this quarter using computer vision.
Real-time scanning flags low inventory instantly, so you never run out.
Shift staff from inventory counts to customer interactions and upselling.
Test a single camera feed on your worst-stocked shelf with zero upfront risk.
Measure time saved this week by installing a camera on one low-stock aisle.
